title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:Mac:kernel-workspace:82416:run:20260404T214351:86053 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/kernel/bootstrap...
summary_text: Decision rubric, weighted to match the real bottleneck: | Criterion | Weight | What “5” means | |---|---:|---| | Token-pressure relief | 30 | materially lowers reload/context injection cost across runs | | Implementation fit | 25 | plugs into Kernel with minimal schema/flow change | | Authority safety | 25 | cannot become a second truth source | | Ops complexity | 10 | easy to run, debug, and bypass | | Cross-provider sharing | 10 | usable by both Claude and Codex without duplicate state | Scoring rule: `score = sum(weight × rating/5)`. Decision bands: `85-100 adopt`, `70-84 pilot`, `55-69 only if no better option`, `<55 reject`. Using that rubric, the candidates score roughly like this: | Option | Token | Fit | Safety | Ops | Sharing | Total | |---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:| | Hermes as narrow read-only sidecar | 3 | 3 | 3 | 2 | 4 | 59 | | Repo-native retrieval improvements | 2 | 5 | 5 | 5 | 2 | 77 | | Shared retrieval DB, separate from orchestrator | 4 | 4 | 4 | 3 | 5 | 81 | | Hermes as shared memory authority | 5 | 1 | 1 | 1 | 4 | 41 | Why Hermes does not win: Hermes’ own docs say its built-in memory is injected into the system prompt at session start, and external providers are also prefetched/injected each turn. So it reduces reload pain, but it does not eliminate prompt cost, and it can still add its own context overhead. Sources: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/memory/ , https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/memory-providers Repo context also argues against making Hermes the authority plane: Kernel already treats [`bootstrap receipt`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-bootstrap-receipt.sh), [`runtime ledger`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh), [`compact artifact`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh), and [`runtime health`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh) as canonical, and [`docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md`](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md) makes external context metadata-only. Bottom line: if the goal is shared memory across Claude/Codex to reduce repeated reload work, the best first choice is a shared retrieval DB or tighter repo-native retrieval, not Hermes as the shared memory plane. Hermes is only a second-step option if you want an agent-native memory UX and accept read-only, advisory-only use.
